description_long: This stone object is an elongated, irregularly shaped rock with a pointed end and a broader, blockier opposite side. Its surface displays a striking pattern of alternating gray and white bands, likely due to natural mineral veining, giving it a marbled appearance. The stone's texture appears rough and uneven, with visible ridges and jagged edges, suggesting it has not been artificially shaped or polished. The top surface is slightly sloped and uneven, while the sides show a mix of flat and angular facets. There is a small metallic tag or label affixed to the upper surface, possibly for identification or cataloging. The overall look is that of a naturally occurring stone, suitable for use as an obstacle or barrier in urban landscaping or park settings.
description: An elongated, rough stone with gray and white marbled veining and jagged, uneven surfaces. It features a pointed end and a metallic tag on top.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stone's pointed end and prominent gray and white banding.
description_view_1: Narrow end view highlighting the stone's thickness and layered texture.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a broader, blockier profile and visible marbling.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the stone's irregular width and the metallic tag.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural marbled veining with irregular gray and white bands running along the length of the stone.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with minor surface irregularities, indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
